def count_stable_segments(levels, m)
  n = levels.length
  count = 0
  
  (0...n).each do |i|
    (i...n).each do |j|
      segment = levels[i..j]
      cost = 0
      max_so_far = segment[0]
      
      segment.each do |level|
        if level < max_so_far
          cost += max_so_far - level
        else
          max_so_far = level
        end
      end
      
      if cost <= m
        count += 1
      end
    end
  end
  
  count
end

def main()
  first_line = gets.chomp
  levels = first_line.split('[')[1].split(']')[0].split(',').map(&:strip).map(&:to_i)
  
  second_line = gets.chomp
  m = second_line.split('=')[1].strip.to_i
  
  result = count_stable_segments(levels, m)
  puts result
end

import sys

class Garden:
    def countStableSegments(self, levels: list[int], m: int) -> int:
        n = len(levels)
        if n == 0:
            return 0
        
        left = 0
        total_stable = 0
        current_m_needed = 0
        stack = []
        
        for right in range(n):
            val = levels[right]
            added_m = 0
            count = 1
            
            while stack and stack[-1][0] > val:
                top_val, top_count = stack.pop()
                added_m += (top_val - val) * top_count
                count += top_count
            
            stack.append((val, count))
            current_m_needed += added_m
            
            while current_m_needed > m and left <= right:
                removed_val = levels[left]
                
                temp_stack = []
                while stack:
                    v, c = stack.pop()
                    if c > 1:
                        stack.append((v, c - 1))
                        break
                
                left += 1
                
                if left <= right:
                    new_first_val = levels[left]
                    search_idx = 0
                    current_sum = 0
                    
                    new_m = 0
                    running_max = levels[left]
                    for i in range(left + 1, right + 1):
                        if levels[i] < running_max:
                            new_m += (running_max - levels[i])
                        else:
                            running_max = levels[i]
                    current_m_needed = new_m
                    
                    new_stack = []
                    if left <= right:
                        curr_max = levels[left]
                        curr_cnt = 1
                        for i in range(left + 1, right + 1):
                            if levels[i] < curr_max:
                                curr_cnt += 1
                            else:
                                new_stack.append((curr_max, curr_cnt))
                                curr_max = levels[i]
                                curr_cnt = 1
                        new_stack.append((curr_max, curr_cnt))
                    stack = new_stack
                else:
                    current_m_needed = 0
                    stack = []
            
            total_stable += (right - left + 1)
            
        return total_stable

    def countStableSegmentsOptimized(self, levels: list[int], m: int) -> int:
        n = len(levels)
        ans = 0
        l = 0
        cost = 0
        dq = []
        
        for r in range(n):
            cnt = 1
            while dq and dq[-1][0] > levels[r]:
                v, c = dq.pop()
                cost += (v - levels[r]) * c
                cnt += c
            dq.append([levels[r], cnt])
            
            while cost > m:
                dq[0][1] -= 1
                cost -= (dq[0][0] - levels[l])
                if dq[0][1] == 0:
                    dq.pop(0)
                l += 1
            ans += (r - l + 1)
        return ans

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    levels_str = ""
    m_val = 0
    
    ptr = 0
    if input_data[0].startswith("levels"):
        ptr = 2
        levels = []
        while ptr < len(input_data) and not input_data[ptr].startswith("m"):
            clean = input_data[ptr].strip("[],")
            if clean:
                levels.append(int(clean))
            ptr += 1
        m_val = int(input_data[-1])
    else:
        levels = [int(x.strip("[],")) for x in input_data[:-1]]
        m_val = int(input_data[-1])

    garden = Garden()
    print(garden.countStableSegmentsOptimized(levels, m_val))

require 'json'

class Garden
  def countStableSegments(levels, m)
    n = levels.length
    ans = 0
    left = 0
    cost = 0
    deque = []

    (0...n).each do |right|
      if right > 0
        while !deque.empty? && levels[deque.last] <= levels[right]
          last_idx = deque.pop
          prev_idx = deque.empty? ? left - 1 : deque.last
          cost += (levels[right] - levels[last_idx]) * (last_idx - prev_idx)
        end
        deque.push(right)
      else
        deque.push(0)
      end

      while cost > m
        cost -= [0, levels[deque.first] - levels[left]].max
        if deque.first == left
          deque.shift
        end
        left += 1
      end
      ans += (right - left + 1)
    end
    ans
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    lines = input_data.split("\n").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    levels_str = lines[0].gsub(/levels\s*=\s*/, '')
    m_str = lines[1].gsub(/m\s*=\s*/, '')
    
    levels = JSON.parse(levels_str)
    m = m_str.to_i
    
    garden = Garden.new
    puts garden.countStableSegments(levels, m)
  rescue
    levels_part = input_data.match(/levels\s*=\s*(\[[^\]]+\])/)
    m_part = input_data.match(/m\s*=\s*(\d+)/)
    if levels_part && m_part
      levels = JSON.parse(levels_part[1])
      m = m_part[1].to_i
      garden = Garden.new
      puts garden.countStableSegments(levels, m)
    end
  end
end

from typing import List
import sys

class Solution:
    def optimalTreasure(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        if not grid or not grid[0]:
            return 0
        
        m, n = len(grid), len(grid[0])
        
        max_treasure = float('-inf')
        
        def get_all_paths():
            paths = []
            def dfs(i, j, path):
                if i == m - 1 and j == n - 1:
                    paths.append(path[:])
                    return
                if i + 1 < m:
                    path.append((i + 1, j))
                    dfs(i + 1, j, path)
                    path.pop()
                if j + 1 < n:
                    path.append((i, j + 1))
                    dfs(i, j + 1, path)
                    path.pop()
            dfs(0, 0, [(0, 0)])
            return paths
        
        paths = get_all_paths()
        
        for path in paths:
            values = [grid[i][j] for i, j in path]
            negative_indices = [(idx, val) for idx, val in enumerate(values) if val < 0]
            
            if len(negative_indices) <= 2:
                treasure = sum(max(0, v) for v in values)
            else:
                negative_indices.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
                skip_set = set([negative_indices[0][0], negative_indices[1][0]])
                treasure = sum(values[i] if i not in skip_set else 0 for i in range(len(values)))
            
            max_treasure = max(max_treasure, treasure)
        
        return max_treasure

        if line.strip():

    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.optimalTreasure(grid)
    print(result)

def optimal_treasure(grid)
    m = grid.length
    return 0 if m == 0
    n = grid[0].length
    return 0 if n == 0
    
    max_treasure = -Float::INFINITY
    
    (0..2).each do |skips|
        dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(skips + 1, -Float::INFINITY) } }
        
        if grid[0][0] < 0 && skips > 0
            dp[0][0][1] = 0
        end
        dp[0][0][0] = grid[0][0]
        
        (0...m).each do |i|
            (0...n).each do |j|
                next if i == 0 && j == 0
                
                (0..skips).each do |k|
                    from_up = -Float::INFINITY
                    from_left = -Float::INFINITY
                    
                    if i > 0
                        from_up = dp[i-1][j][k]
                    end
                    
                    if j > 0
                        from_left = dp[i][j-1][k]
                    end
                    
                    best_prev = [from_up, from_left].max
                    
                    if best_prev > -Float::INFINITY
                        dp[i][j][k] = [dp[i][j][k], best_prev + grid[i][j]].max
                    end
                    
                    if grid[i][j] < 0 && k > 0
                        from_up_skip = -Float::INFINITY
                        from_left_skip = -Float::INFINITY
                        
                        if i > 0
                            from_up_skip = dp[i-1][j][k-1]
                        end
                        
                        if j > 0
                            from_left_skip = dp[i][j-1][k-1]
                        end
                        
                        best_prev_skip = [from_up_skip, from_left_skip].max
                        
                        if best_prev_skip > -Float::INFINITY
                            dp[i][j][k] = [dp[i][j][k], best_prev_skip].max
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        (0..skips).each do |k|
            max_treasure = [max_treasure, dp[m-1][n-1][k]].max
        end
    end
    
    max_treasure
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def optimalTreasure(grid)
    m = grid.length
    n = grid[0].length
    
    dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(3, -Float::INFINITY) } }
    
    val = grid[0][0]
    if val < 0
      dp[0][0][0] = val
      dp[0][0][1] = 0
    else
      dp[0][0][0] = val
      dp[0][0][1] = val
    end
    dp[0][0][2] = dp[0][0][1]

    (0...m).each do |i|
      (0...n).each do |j|
        next if i == 0 && j == 0
        
        val = grid[i][j]
        
        [0, 1, 2].each do |k|
          prev_best = -Float::INFINITY
          if i > 0
            prev_best = [prev_best, dp[i-1][j][k]].max
          end
          if j > 0
            prev_best = [prev_best, dp[i][j-1][k]].max
          end
          
          if prev_best != -Float::INFINITY
            dp[i][j][k] = [dp[i][j][k], prev_best + val].max
          end
          
          if k > 0 && val < 0
            prev_best_k_minus = -Float::INFINITY
            if i > 0
              prev_best_k_minus = [prev_best_k_minus, dp[i-1][j][k-1]].max
            end
            if j > 0
              prev_best_k_minus = [prev_best_k_minus, dp[i][j-1][k-1]].max
            end
            
            if prev_best_k_minus != -Float::INFINITY
              dp[i][j][k] = [dp[i][j][k], prev_best_k_minus].max
            end
          elsif k > 0 && val >= 0
            prev_best_k_minus = -Float::INFINITY
            if i > 0
              prev_best_k_minus = [prev_best_k_minus, dp[i-1][j][k-1]].max
            end
            if j > 0
              prev_best_k_minus = [prev_best_k_minus, dp[i][j-1][k-1]].max
            end
            if prev_best_k_minus != -Float::INFINITY
              dp[i][j][k] = [dp[i][j][k], prev_best_k_minus + val].max
            end
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    dp[m-1][n-1].max
  end
end

def main()
    n = gets.to_i
    routes = []
    (n - 1).times do
        routes << gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    end
    attractions = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    
    adj = Array.new(n) { [] }
    routes.each do |u, v, w|
        adj[u] << [v, w]
        adj[v] << [u, w]
    end
    
    max_dist = 0
    min_stations = 0
    
    def dfs(node, parent, adj, attractions, visited, dist, count)
        results = [[dist, count]]
        
        adj[node].each do |neighbor, weight|
            next if neighbor == parent
            next if visited[attractions[neighbor]]
            
            visited[attractions[neighbor]] = true
            child_results = dfs(neighbor, node, adj, attractions, visited, dist + weight, count + 1)
            results.concat(child_results)
            visited[attractions[neighbor]] = false
        end
        
        results
    end
    
    (0...n).each do |start|
        visited = {}
        visited[attractions[start]] = true
        results = dfs(start, -1, adj, attractions, visited, 0, 1)
        
        results.each do |d, c|
            if d > max_dist || (d == max_dist && c < min_stations)
                max_dist = d
                min_stations = c
            end
        end
    end
    
    puts "#{max_dist} #{min_stations}"
end

import sys

sys.setrecursionlimit(200000)

class Odyssey:
    def longestAdventureRoute(self, routes, attractions):
        n = len(attractions)
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for u, v, w in routes:
            adj[u].append((v, w))
            adj[v].append((u, w))
        
        self.max_dist = 0
        self.min_nodes = 1
        
        current_path_nodes = []
        current_path_dist = [0]
        seen_types = {}
        
        def dfs(u, p, d):
            node_type = attractions[u]
            
            prev_idx = seen_types.get(node_type, -1)
            
            start_idx = prev_idx + 1
            path_len = d - current_path_dist[start_idx]
            num_nodes = len(current_path_nodes) - start_idx + 1
            
            if path_len > self.max_dist:
                self.max_dist = path_len
                self.min_nodes = num_nodes
            elif path_len == self.max_dist:
                if num_nodes < self.min_nodes:
                    self.min_nodes = num_nodes
            
            seen_types[node_type] = len(current_path_nodes)
            current_path_nodes.append(u)
            current_path_dist.append(d)
            
            for v, w in adj[u]:
                if v != p:
                    dfs(v, u, d + w)
            
            current_path_nodes.pop()
            current_path_dist.pop()
            if prev_idx == -1:
                del seen_types[node_type]
            else:
                seen_types[node_type] = prev_idx

        dfs(0, -1, 0)
        return [self.max_dist, self.min_nodes]

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    n = int(input_data[0])
    idx = 1
    routes = []
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        u = int(input_data[idx])
        v = int(input_data[idx+1])
        w = int(input_data[idx+2])
        routes.append([u, v, w])
        idx += 3
    
    attractions = []
    for _ in range(n):
        attractions.append(int(input_data[idx]))
        idx += 1
    
    sol = Odyssey()
    result = sol.longestAdventureRoute(routes, attractions)
    print(f"{result[0]} {result[1]}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

require 'json'

class Odyssey
  def longestAdventureRoute(routes, attractions)
    n = attractions.size
    adj = Array.new(n) { [] }
    routes.each do |u, v, d|
      adj[u] << [v, d]
      adj[v] << [u, d]
    end

    @max_dist = -1
    @min_nodes = Float::INFINITY
    @attractions = attractions
    @adj = adj
    @current_path_types = {}

    dfs(0, -1, 0, 0)
    
    [@max_dist, @min_nodes]
  end

  def dfs(u, p, current_dist, current_nodes)
    type = @attractions[u]
    
    if @current_path_types.key?(type)
      return
    end

    @current_path_types[type] = true
    new_dist = current_dist
    new_nodes = current_nodes + 1

    update_ans(new_dist, new_nodes)

    @adj[u].each do |v, d|
      next if v == p
      dfs_from_start(v, u, d, 2)
    end

    @adj[u].each do |v, d|
      next if v == p
      dfs(v, u, 0, 0)
    end

    @current_path_types.delete(type)
  end

  def dfs_from_start(u, p, current_dist, current_nodes)
    type = @attractions[u]
    if @current_path_types.key?(type)
      return
    end

    @current_path_types[type] = true
    update_ans(current_dist, current_nodes)

    @adj[u].each do |v, d|
      next if v == p
      dfs_from_start(v, u, current_dist + d, current_nodes + 1)
    end

    @current_path_types.delete(type)
  end

  def update_ans(dist, nodes)
    if dist > @max_dist
      @max_dist = dist
      @min_nodes = nodes
    elsif dist == @max_dist
      @min_nodes = nodes if nodes < @min_nodes
    end
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse(input_data)
    routes = parsed["routes"]
    attractions = parsed["attractions"]
    
    solver = Odyssey.new
    result = solver.longestAdventureRoute(routes, attractions)
    puts JSON.generate(result)
  rescue
    lines = input_data.split("\n")
    return if lines.empty?
    routes = JSON.parse(lines[0])
    attractions = JSON.parse(lines[1])
    solver = Odyssey.new
    result = solver.longestAdventureRoute(routes, attractions)
    puts JSON.generate(result)
  end
end

def shortest_valid_route_segment(map_str, pattern)
  hash_positions = []
  pattern.chars.each_with_index do |c, i|
    if c == '#'
      hash_positions << i
    end
  end
  
  first_hash = hash_positions[0]
  second_hash = hash_positions[1]
  
  prefix = pattern[0...first_hash]
  middle = pattern[first_hash + 1...second_hash]
  suffix = pattern[second_hash + 1..-1]
  
  min_length = Float::INFINITY
  
  (0..map_str.length).each do |start|
    (start..map_str.length).each do |finish|
      segment = map_str[start...finish]
      
      next if segment.length < prefix.length + middle.length + suffix.length
      
      if !segment.start_with?(prefix)
        next
      end
      
      if !segment.end_with?(suffix)
        next
      end
      
      remaining_after_prefix = segment[prefix.length..-1]
      
      if suffix.empty?
        remaining_before_suffix = remaining_after_prefix
      else
        if remaining_after_prefix.length < suffix.length
          next
        end
        remaining_before_suffix = remaining_after_prefix[0...-suffix.length]
      end
      
      middle_pos = remaining_before_suffix.index(middle)
      if middle_pos.nil? && !middle.empty?
        next
      end
      
      if middle.empty? || middle_pos >= 0
        if segment.length < min_length
          min_length = segment.length
        end
      end
    end
  end
  
  if min_length == Float::INFINITY
    return -1
  else
    return min_length
  end
end

def main()
  map_str = gets.chomp
  pattern = gets.chomp
  result = shortest_valid_route_segment(map_str, pattern)
  puts result
end

class RouteAnalyzer
  def shortestValidRouteSegment(map_str, pattern)
    parts = pattern.split('#', -1)
    p1, p2, p3 = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2]
    
    if p1.empty? && p2.empty? && p3.empty?
      return 0
    end

    n = map_str.length
    
    def find_all_occurrences(text, sub)
      return [] if sub.nil?
      indices = []
      return indices if sub.empty?
      i = 0
      while i <= text.length - sub.length
        idx = text.index(sub, i)
        break if idx.nil?
        indices << idx
        i = idx + 1
      end
      indices
    end

    if p1.empty? && p2.empty? && p3.empty?
      return 0
    end

    occ1 = p1.empty? ? [0] : find_all_occurrences(map_str, p1)
    occ2 = p2.empty? ? (0..n).to_a : find_all_occurrences(map_str, p2)
    occ3 = p3.empty? ? (0..n).to_a : find_all_occurrences(map_str, p3)

    return -1 if occ1.empty? || occ2.empty? || occ3.empty?

    best_len = Float::INFINITY

    if p1.empty? && p3.empty?
      if p2.empty?
        return 0
      else
        return p2.length
      end
    end

    p2_idx = 0
    p3_idx = 0
    
    occ1.each do |i1|
      while p2_idx < occ2.length && occ2[p2_idx] < i1 + p1.length
        p2_idx += 1
      end
      next if p2_idx == occ2.length
      
      i2 = occ2[p2_idx]
      
      while p3_idx < occ3.length && occ3[p3_idx] < i2 + p2.length
        p3_idx += 1
      end
      next if p3_idx == occ3.length
      
      i3 = occ3[p3_idx]
      
      current_len = (i3 + p3.length) - i1
      best_len = [best_len, current_len].min
    end

    best_len == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : best_len
  end
end

def main
  input_data = $stdin.read.split("\n")
  return if input_data.empty?
  
  map_str = ""
  pattern = ""
  
  input_data.each do |line|
    if line.start_with?("map = ")
      map_str = line.split('"')[1]
    elsif line.start_with?("pattern = ")
      pattern = line.split('"')[1]
    end
  end

  if map_str.empty? || pattern.empty?
    raw = input_data.join(" ")
    m = raw.match(/map\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"/)
    map_str = m[1] if m
    p = raw.match(/pattern\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"/)
    pattern = p[1] if p
  end

  analyzer = RouteAnalyzer.new
  puts analyzer.shortestValidRouteSegment(map_str, pattern)
end

  code = gets.chomp
  result = compute_valid_segments(code)
  puts result

def compute_valid_segments(code)
  n = code.length
  count = 0
  
  (0...n).each do |i|
    num = 0
    (i...n).each do |j|
      digit = code[j].to_i
      num = num * 10 + digit
      
      if digit != 0 && num % digit == 0
        count += 1
      end
    end
  end
  
  count
end

class SecurityEvaluator
  def computeValidSegments(code)
    n = code.length
    total_valid = 0
    
    digits = code.chars.map(&:to_i)
    
    mods = Array.new(10) { |d| Array.new(d, 0) }
    
    digits.each do |d|
      if d > 0
        new_mods = Array.new(10) { |x| Array.new(x, 0) }
        (1..9).each do |m|
          (0...m).each do |rem|
            count = mods[m][rem]
            if count > 0
              new_rem = (rem * 10 + d) % m
              new_mods[m][new_rem] += count
            end
          end
          new_mods[m][d % m] += 1
        end
        mods = new_mods
        total_valid += mods[d][0]
      else
        new_mods = Array.new(10) { |x| Array.new(x, 0) }
        (1..9).each do |m|
          (0...m).each do |rem|
            count = mods[m][rem]
            if count > 0
              new_rem = (rem * 10) % m
              new_mods[m][new_rem] += count
            end
          end
          new_mods[m][0] += 1
        end
        mods = new_mods
      end
    end
    
    total_valid
  end
end

class Tracker:
    def minimumUpgrades(self, scores: List[int], milestones: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(scores)
        m = len(milestones)
        
        min_cost = float('inf')
        
        for perm in permutations(range(n), m):
            cost = 0
            valid = True
            for i, milestone in enumerate(milestones):
                score_idx = perm[i]
                score = scores[score_idx]
                next_multiple = ((score + milestone - 1)
                cost += next_multiple - score
            min_cost = min(min_cost, cost)
        
        return min_cost

def solve(scores, milestones)
  n = scores.length
  m = milestones.length
  
  min_moves = Float::INFINITY
  
  (0...(1 << m)).each do |mask|
    selected = []
    m.times do |i|
      selected << milestones[i] if (mask & (1 << i)) != 0
    end
    
    next if selected.empty?
    
    lcm_val = selected[0]
    selected.each do |val|
      lcm_val = lcm_val.lcm(val)
    end
    
    remaining = milestones.reject.with_index { |_, i| (mask & (1 << i)) != 0 }
    
    def backtrack(scores, milestones, idx, assigned, memo)
      key = [idx, assigned.clone]
      return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
      
      if idx == milestones.length
        total = 0
        assigned.each do |score_idx, milestone_indices|
          score = scores[score_idx]
          needed_lcm = 1
          milestone_indices.each do |mi|
            needed_lcm = needed_lcm.lcm(milestones[mi])
          end
          
          next_multiple = ((score + needed_lcm - 1) / needed_lcm) * needed_lcm
          total += next_multiple - score
        end
        memo[key] = total
        return total
      end
      
      min_cost = Float::INFINITY
      scores.length.times do |si|
        assigned[si] ||= []
        assigned[si] << idx
        cost = backtrack(scores, milestones, idx + 1, assigned, memo)
        min_cost = [min_cost, cost].min
        assigned[si].pop
        assigned.delete(si) if assigned[si].empty?
      end
      
      memo[key] = min_cost
      min_cost
    end
    
    if remaining.empty?
      min_cost = scores.map do |score|
        next_multiple = ((score + lcm_val - 1) / lcm_val) * lcm_val
        next_multiple - score
      end.min
      
      min_moves = [min_moves, min_cost].min
    else
      available_scores = scores.reject.with_index do |score, i|
        next_multiple = ((score + lcm_val - 1) / lcm_val) * lcm_val
        next_multiple - score
      end
      
      min_cost_for_lcm = scores.map.with_index do |score, i|
        next_multiple = ((score + lcm_val - 1) / lcm_val) * lcm_val
        [next_multiple - score, i]
      end.min
      
      cost_lcm = min_cost_for_lcm[0]
      
      remaining_scores = scores.reject.with_index { |_, i| i == min_cost_for_lcm[1] }
      
      if remaining_scores.length >= remaining.length
        memo = {}
        cost_remaining = backtrack(remaining_scores, remaining, 0, {}, memo)
        min_moves = [min_moves, cost_lcm + cost_remaining].min
      end
    end
  end
  
  memo = {}
  cost_all_separate = backtrack(scores, milestones, 0, {}, memo)
  min_moves = [min_moves, cost_all_separate].min
  
  min_moves
end

  scores = gets.strip.split.map(&:to_i)
  milestones = gets.strip.split.map(&:to_i)
  
  result = solve(scores, milestones)
  puts result
end

def minimal_change_ideal_inscription(inscription)
    n = inscription.length
    
    if n < 3
        return ""
    end
    
    min_cost = Float::INFINITY
    best_result = nil
    
    def try_partition(inscription, start, current_partition, all_partitions)
        n = inscription.length
        if start == n
            all_partitions << current_partition.dup
            return
        end
        
        (3..(n - start)).each do |len|
            break if start + len > n
            current_partition << [start, start + len - 1]
            try_partition(inscription, start + len, current_partition, all_partitions)
            current_partition.pop
        end
    end
    
    all_partitions = []
    try_partition(inscription, 0, [], all_partitions)
    
    all_partitions.each do |partition|
        total_cost = 0
        result_chars = inscription.chars.dup
        
        partition.each do |start_idx, end_idx|
            group_chars = inscription[start_idx..end_idx].chars
            
            best_char = nil
            best_group_cost = Float::INFINITY
            
            ('a'..'z').each do |target|
                cost = group_chars.sum { |c| (c.ord - target.ord).abs }
                if cost < best_group_cost || (cost == best_group_cost && (best_char.nil? || target < best_char))
                    best_group_cost = cost
                    best_char = target
                end
            end
            
            total_cost += best_group_cost
            (start_idx..end_idx).each { |i| result_chars[i] = best_char }
        end
        
        result_str = result_chars.join
        
        if total_cost < min_cost || (total_cost == min_cost && (best_result.nil? || result_str < best_result))
            min_cost = total_cost
            best_result = result_str
        end
    end
    
    best_result.nil? ? "" : best_result
end

import sys

class Solution:
    def minimalChangeIdealInscription(self, inscription: str) -> str:
        n = len(inscription)
        if n < 3:
            return ""
        
        inf = float('inf')
        dp = [inf] * (n + 1)
        dp[0] = 0
        
        parent = [-1] * (n + 1)
        parent_char = [''] * (n + 1)
        
        s_idx = [ord(c) - ord('a') for c in inscription]

        for i in range(n):
            if dp[i] == inf:
                continue
            
            for length in range(3, 6):
                if i + length > n:
                    break
                
                for char_code in range(26):
                    cost = 0
                    for k in range(i, i + length):
                        cost += abs(s_idx[k] - char_code)
                    
                    new_cost = dp[i] + cost
                    if new_cost < dp[i + length]:
                        dp[i + length] = new_cost
                        parent[i + length] = i
                        parent_char[i + length] = chr(ord('a') + char_code)
                    elif new_cost == dp[i + length]:
                        current_path = []
                        curr = i + length
                        p = i
                        c = chr(ord('a') + char_code)
                        
                        temp_res = []
                        temp_curr = i + length
                        temp_p = i
                        temp_c = chr(ord('a') + char_code)
                        
                        def get_string(idx):
                            res = []
                            curr_idx = idx
                            while curr_idx > 0:
                                p_idx = parent[curr_idx]
                                char = parent_char[curr_idx]
                                res.append(char * (curr_idx - p_idx))
                                curr_idx = p_idx
                            return "".join(reversed(res))

                        old_s = get_string(i + length)
                        
                        new_suffix = chr(ord('a') + char_code) * length
                        prefix_s = get_string(i)
                        new_s = prefix_s + new_suffix
                        
                        if new_s < old_s:
                            parent[i + length] = i
                            parent_char[i + length] = chr(ord('a') + char_code)

        if dp[n] == inf:
            return ""
        
        res_parts = []
        curr = n
        while curr > 0:
            p = parent[curr]
            char = parent_char[curr]
            res_parts.append(char * (curr - p))
            curr = p
        
        return "".join(reversed(res_parts))

    if not input_data:

    sol = Solution()
    print(sol.minimalChangeIdealInscription(inscription))

class Solution
  def minimalChangeIdealInscription(inscription)
    n = inscription.length
    return "" if n < 3

    inf = Float::INFINITY
    dp = Array.new(n + 1) { [inf, ''] }
    dp[0] = [0, '']

    (3..n).each do |i|
      best_cost = inf
      best_char = ''
      best_prev_len = -1

      ('a'..'z').each do |char|
        char_code = char.ord
        
        [3, 4, 5].each do |k|
          prev_idx = i - k
          next if prev_idx < 0
          next if dp[prev_idx][0] == inf

          current_cost = 0
          (prev_idx...i).each do |j|
            current_cost += (inscription[j].ord - char_code).abs
          end
          
          total_cost = dp[prev_idx][0] + current_cost
          new_str_part = char * k
          
          if total_cost < best_cost
            best_cost = total_cost
            best_char = char
            best_prev_len = k
          elsif total_cost == best_cost
            if best_char == '' || char < best_char
              best_char = char
              best_prev_len = k
            end
          end
        end
      end
      dp[i] = [best_cost, best_char, best_prev_len]
    end

    return "" if dp[n][0] == inf

    res = ""
    curr = n
    while curr > 0
      cost, char, len = dp[curr]
      res = (char * len) + res
      curr -= len
    end
    res
  end
end

  input = gets
  if input
    inscription = input.strip
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.minimalChangeIdealInscription(inscription)
  end
end

class Solution:
    def countTeamSplits(self, scores: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(scores)
        count = 0
        
        for j in range(n - 1):
            first_sum = sum(scores[:j + 1])
            second_sum = sum(scores[j + 1:])
            diff = first_sum - second_sum
            
            if diff % 2 == 0:
                count += 1
        
        return count

class Solution
  def countTeamSplits(scores)
    n = scores.length
    total_sum = scores.sum
    count = 0
    prefix_sum = 0
    (0...n-1).each do |j|
      prefix_sum += scores[j]
      suffix_sum = total_sum - prefix_sum
      diff = prefix_sum - suffix_sum
      count += 1 if diff.even?
    end
    count
  end
end

class Solution
  def findUniqueSignal(signals, w)
    n = signals.length
    window_count = Hash.new(0)
    
    (0..(n - w)).each do |i|
      window = signals[i, w]
      unique_in_window = window.uniq
      unique_in_window.each do |val|
        window_count[val] += 1
      end
    end
    
    candidates = window_count.select { |k, v| v == 1 }.keys
    return -1 if candidates.empty?
    candidates.max
  end
end

def find_uniform_segment(sequence, n)
  (0..sequence.length - n).each do |i|
    segment = sequence[i, n]
    
    if segment.chars.all? { |c| c == segment[0] }
      before_ok = i == 0 || sequence[i - 1] != segment[0]
      after_ok = i + n >= sequence.length || sequence[i + n] != segment[0]
      
      return true if before_ok && after_ok
    end
  end
  
  false
end

  sequence = gets.chomp
  n = gets.chomp.to_i
  
  result = find_uniform_segment(sequence, n)
  puts result
end

class Solution
  def findUniformSegment(sequence, n)
    len = sequence.length
    (0..len - n).each do |i|
      segment = sequence[i, n]
      char = segment[0]
      if segment == char * n
        before_ok = (i == 0 || sequence[i - 1] != char)
        after_ok = (i + n == len || sequence[i + n] != char)
        return true if before_ok && after_ok
      end
    end
    false
  end
end

class DeviceAnalyzer:
    def computeExtremeSum(self, scores, maxCount):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(scores)
        scores.sort()
        
        fact = [1] * (n + 1)
        inv = [1] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            fact[i] = (fact[i - 1] * i) % MOD
        
        inv[n] = pow(fact[n], MOD - 2, MOD)
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            inv[i] = (inv[i + 1] * (i + 1)) % MOD
            
        def nCr_mod(n, r):
            if r < 0 or r > n:
                return 0
            num = fact[n]
            den = (inv[r] * inv[n - r]) % MOD
            return (num * den) % MOD

        total_min = 0
        total_max = 0
        
        comb_sums = [0] * (n + 1)
        current_sum = 0
        for i in range(n):
            current_sum = 0
            for k in range(1, maxCount + 1):
                current_sum = (current_sum + nCr_mod(i, k - 1)) % MOD
            comb_sums[i] = current_sum

        for i in range(n):
            ways_as_max = comb_sums[i]
            total_max = (total_max + scores[i] * ways_as_max) % MOD
            
            ways_as_min = comb_sums[n - 1 - i]
            total_min = (total_min + scores[i] * ways_as_min) % MOD
            
        return (total_min + total_max) % MOD

require 'json'

class DeviceAnalyzer
  MOD = 10**9 + 7

  def computeExtremeSum(scores, max_count)
    n = scores.length
    scores.sort!
    
    fact = Array.new(n + 1)
    inv_fact = Array.new(n + 1)
    fact[0] = 1
    (1..n).each { |i| fact[i] = (fact[i - 1] * i) % MOD }
    inv_fact[n] = power(fact[n], MOD - 2)
    (n - 1).downto(0).each { |i| inv_fact[i] = (inv_fact[i + 1] * (i + 1)) % MOD }

    def nCr(n, r, fact, inv_fact)
      return 0 if r < 0 || r > n
      num = fact[n]
      den = (inv_fact[r] * inv_fact[n - r]) % MOD
      (num * den) % MOD
    end

    total_max = 0
    total_min = 0

    (0...n).each do |i|
      ways_max = 0
      ways_min = 0
      
      limit = [i, max_count - 1].min
      (0..limit).each do |k|
        ways_max = (ways_max + nCr(i, k, fact, inv_fact)) % MOD
      end
      
      limit_min = [n - 1 - i, max_count - 1].min
      (0..limit_min).each do |k|
        ways_min = (ways_min + nCr(n - 1 - i, k, fact, inv_fact)) % MOD
      end
      
      total_max = (total_max + scores[i] * ways_max) % MOD
      total_min = (total_min + scores[i] * ways_min) % MOD
    end

    (total_max + total_min) % MOD
  end

  private

  def power(a, b)
    res = 1
    a %= MOD
    while b > 0
      res = (res * a) % MOD if b.odd?
      a = (a * a) % MOD
      b /= 2
    end
    res
  end
end

def main()
    levels = gets.chomp.split(',').map(&:to_i)
    groupSize = gets.chomp.to_i
    groupCount = gets.chomp.to_i
    
    n = levels.length
    
    def cost_to_unify(arr, start, size)
        segment = arr[start, size]
        sorted_seg = segment.sort
        median = sorted_seg[size / 2]
        segment.sum { |x| (x - median).abs }
    end
    
    dp = Array.new(groupCount + 1) { Array.new(n + 1, Float::INFINITY) }
    dp[0][0] = 0
    
    for i in 1..groupCount
        for j in 0..n
            dp[i][j] = dp[i][j - 1] if j > 0
            
            if j >= groupSize
                start = j - groupSize
                cost = cost_to_unify(levels, start, groupSize)
                prev_cost = (start > 0) ? dp[i - 1][start] : (i == 1 ? 0 : Float::INFINITY)
                dp[i][j] = [dp[i][j], prev_cost + cost].min
            end
        end
    end
    
    puts dp[groupCount][n]
end

def main()
    hubs_count = gets.to_i
    connections_count = gets.to_i
    connections = []
    connections_count.times do
        connections << gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    end
    allowance = gets.to_i
    
    costs = connections.map { |c| c[2] }.uniq.sort
    
    left = 0
    right = costs.length - 1
    result = -1
    
    while left <= right
        mid = (left + right) / 2
        max_cost = costs[mid]
        
        filtered = connections.select { |c| c[2] <= max_cost }
        
        graph = Array.new(hubs_count) { [] }
        out_degree = Array.new(hubs_count, 0)
        
        filtered.each do |source, dest, cost|
            graph[source] << [dest, cost]
        end
        
        graph.each_with_index do |edges, node|
            edges.sort_by! { |e| e[1] }
        end
        
        can_reach = Array.new(hubs_count, false)
        can_reach[0] = true
        
        max_iterations = hubs_count
        max_iterations.times do
            changed = false
            hubs_count.times do |node|
                next if can_reach[node]
                
                available_edges = graph[node].select { |dest, cost| can_reach[dest] }
                
                if available_edges.length > 0
                    can_reach[node] = true
                    changed = true
                end
            end
            break unless changed
        end
        
        if can_reach.all?
            result = max_cost
            right = mid - 1
        else
            left = mid + 1
        end
    end
    
    puts result
end

require 'json'

def solve
  input_data = $stdin.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.strip.empty?
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse("[" + input_data.gsub(/(\w+)\s*:/, '"\1":').gsub("'", '"') + "]")
    hubs_count = parsed[0]
    connections = parsed[1]
    allowance = parsed[2]
  rescue
    parts = input_data.split(/\n/)
    hubs_count = parts[0].to_i
    connections = JSON.parse(parts[1])
    allowance = parts[2].to_i
  end

  adj = Array.new(hubs_count) { [] }
  costs = []
  connections.each do |u, v, w|
    adj[u] << [v, w]
    costs << w
  end
  costs.uniq!.sort!

  def can_reach_zero(hubs_count, adj, max_w)
    rev_adj = Array.new(hubs_count) { [] }
    hubs_count.times do |u|
      adj[u].each do |v, w|
        rev_adj[v] << u if w <= max_w
      end
    end

    visited = Array.new(hubs_count, false)
    queue = [0]
    visited[0] = true
    head = 0
    while head < queue.length
      u = queue[head]
      head += 1
      rev_adj[u].each do |v|
        unless visited[v]
          visited[v] = true
          queue << v
        end
      end
    end
    visited.all?
  end

  ans = -1
  low = 0
  high = costs.length - 1
  while low <= high
    mid = (low + high) / 2
    if can_reach_zero(hubs_count, adj, costs[mid])
      ans = costs[mid]
      high = mid - 1
    else
      low = mid + 1
    end
  end

  puts ans
end

def main
  solve
end

                total_attendees.times do |i|
                    counts[i] += 1

                total_attendees.times do |i|
                    if timestamp >= absent_until[i]
                        counts[i] += 1
                    end

            else
                tokens = mentions_info.split(' ')
                tokens.each do |token|
                    if token.start_with?("attendee")
                        attendee_id = token[8..-1].to_i
                        counts[attendee_id] += 1
                    end
                end
            end
        end

def solve
  input = STDIN.read
  return if input.nil? || input.empty?
  
  begin
    data = JSON.parse(input)
  rescue
    return
  end
  
  total_attendees = data["totalAttendees"]
  events = data["events"]
  
  counts = Array.new(total_attendees, 0)
  absent_until = Array.new(total_attendees, 0)
  
  sorted_events = events.sort_by do |e|
    [e[1].to_i, e[0] == "ANNOUNCEMENT" ? 1 : 0]
  end
  
  sorted_events.each do |event|
    type = event[0]
    timestamp = event[1].to_i
    info = event[2]
    
    if type == "ABSENT"
      id = info.to_i
      absent_until[id] = timestamp + 60
    elsif type == "ANNOUNCEMENT"
      tokens = info.split(" ")
      tokens.each do |token|
        if token == "EVERYONE"
          (0...total_attendees).each { |i| counts[i] += 1 }
        elsif token == "PRESENT"
          (0...total_attendees).each do |i|
            counts[i] += 1 if absent_until[i] <= timestamp
          end
        elsif token.start_with?("attendee")
          id = token[8..-1].to_i
          counts[id] += 1
        end
      end
    end
  end
  
  puts counts.to_json
end

  scores = gets.chomp.split.map(&:i)
  p = gets.to_i
  n = gets.to_i
  
  len = scores.length
  
  prefix = Array.new(len + 1, 0)
  (0...len).each do |i|
    prefix[i + 1] = prefix[i] + scores[i]
  end
  
  dp = Array.new(p + 1) { Array.new(len + 1, -Float::INFINITY) }
  dp[0][0] = 0
  
  (0..p).each do |segs|
    (0..len).each do |pos|
      next if dp[segs][pos] == -Float::INFINITY
      
      if segs < p
        (n..(len - pos)).each do |seg_len|
          next_pos = pos + seg_len
          seg_sum = prefix[next_pos] - prefix[pos]
          if dp[segs + 1][next_pos] < dp[segs][pos] + seg_sum
            dp[segs + 1][next_pos] = dp[segs][pos] + seg_sum
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end
  
  result = -Float::INFINITY
  (0..len).each do |pos|
    result = [result, dp[p][pos]].max
  end
  
  puts result
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def maxGameSegmentsSum(scores, p, n)
    m = scores.length
    prefix_sum = Array.new(m + 1, 0)
    (0...m).each { |i| prefix_sum[i + 1] = prefix_sum[i] + scores[i] }

    dp = Array.new(p + 1) { Array.new(m + 1, -Float::INFINITY) }
    (0..m).each { |j| dp[0][j] = 0 }

    (1..p).each do |i|
      best_prev_dp = -Float::INFINITY
      (i * n..m).each do |j|
        current_segment_sum = prefix_sum[j] - prefix_sum[j - n]
        potential_start_val = dp[i - 1][j - n]
        
        best_prev_dp = [best_prev_dp + scores[j - 1], potential_start_val + current_segment_sum].max
        
        dp[i][j] = [dp[i][j - 1], best_prev_dp].max
      end
    end

    dp[p][m].to_i
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
    return
  end
  
  begin
    lines = input_data.split("\n")
    scores_line = lines.find { |l| l.include?("scores =") }
    p_line = lines.find { |l| l.include?("p =") }
    n_line = lines.find { |l| l.include?("n =") }

    scores = JSON.parse(scores_line.split("=")[1].strip.chop)
    p_val = p_line.split("=")[1].strip.to_i
    n_val = n_line.split("=")[1].strip.to_i

    sol = Solution.new
    puts Solution.new.class.instance_method(:maxGameSegmentsSum).bind(sol).call(scores, p_val, n_val)
  rescue
    parts = input_data.scan(/\[.*?\]|\d+|-?\d+/)
    if parts.length >= 3
      scores = JSON.parse(parts[0])
      p_val = parts[-2].to_i
      n_val = parts[-1].to_i
      sol = Solution.new
      puts Solution.new.class.instance_method(:maxGameSegmentsSum).bind(sol).call(scores, p_val, n_val)
    end
  end
end

def aggregate_peak_scores(score_list, d)
  total = 0
  n = score_list.length
  
  (0...n).each do |i|
    left_valid = (i - d) >= 0
    right_valid = (i + d) < n
    
    if left_valid && right_valid
      if score_list[i] > score_list[i - d] && score_list[i] > score_list[i + d]
        total += score_list[i]
      end
    elsif left_valid
      if score_list[i] > score_list[i - d]
        total += score_list[i]
      end
    elsif right_valid
      if score_list[i] > score_list[i + d]
        total += score_list[i]
      end
    else
      total += score_list[i]
    end
  end
  
  total
end

  d = gets.strip.to_i
  
  result = aggregate_peak_scores(score_list, d)
  puts result

      subarray = chest_values[i..j]
      min_val = subarray.min
      max_val = subarray.max
      total += min_val + max_val
    end

class TreasureSolution
  def calculateExtremeGemSum(chestValues, limit)
    n = chestValues.length
    total_sum = 0
    
    total_sum += calculate_sliding_sum(chestValues, limit, :min)
    total_sum += calculate_sliding_sum(chestValues, limit, :max)
    
    total_sum
  end

  private

  def calculate_sliding_sum(arr, limit, type)
    n = arr.length
    sum = 0
    deque = []
    
    (1..limit).each do |k|
      deque = []
      (0...n).each do |i|
        if !deque.empty? && deque.first <= i - k
          deque.shift
        end
        
        while !deque.empty? && (type == :min ? arr[deque.last] >= arr[i] : arr[deque.last] <= arr[i])
          deque.pop
        end
        
        deque.push(i)
        
        if i >= k - 1
          sum += arr[deque.first]
        end
      end
    end
    sum
  end
end

def solve_optimized(arr, limit)
  n = arr.length
  
  def get_contribution(arr, n, limit, type)
    total = 0
    arr.each { |x| total += x }
    
    stack = []
    left = Array.new(n, -1)
    (0...n).each do |i|
      while !stack.empty? && (type == :max ? arr[stack.last] <= arr[i] : arr[stack.last] >= arr[i])
        stack.pop
      end
      left[i] = stack.empty? ? -1 : stack.last
      stack.push(i)
    end
    
    stack = []
    right = Array.new(n, n)
    (n - 1).downto(0).each do |i|
      while !stack.empty? && (type == :max ? arr[stack.last] < arr[i] : arr[stack.last] > arr[i])
        stack.pop
      end
      right[i] = stack.empty? ? n : stack.last
      stack.push(i)
    end
    
    res = 0
    (0...n).each do |i|
      l_bound = left[i]
      r_bound = right[i]
      
      a = i - l_bound
      b = r_bound - i
      
      val = arr[i]
      
      count = 0
      if a > b
        a, b = b, a
      end
      
      if limit <= a
        count = limit * (limit + 1) / 2
      elsif limit <= b
        count = a * (a + 1) / 2 + a * (limit - a)
      elsif limit < a + b - 1
        count = a * (a + 1) / 2 + a * (b - a) + (a + (a - (limit - b) + 1)) * (limit - b) / 2
      else
        count = a * b
      end
      res += val * count
    end
    res
  end

  get_contribution(arr, n, limit, :max) + get_contribution(arr, n, limit, :min)
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read.split
  return if input_data.empty?
  
  limit = input_data.pop.to_i
  chest_values = input_data.map(&:to_i)
  
  puts solve_optimized(chest_values, limit)
end

    layout = []
    n.times do
        row = gets.strip.split.map(&:to_i)
        layout << row

    result = compute_longest_z_route(layout)

def compute_longest_z_route(layout)
    return 0 if layout.nil? || layout.empty?
    
    rows = layout.length
    cols = layout[0].length
    max_length = 0
    
    directions = [
        [1, 1],
        [1, -1],
        [-1, 1],
        [-1, -1]
    ]
    
    (0...rows).each do |r|
        (0...cols).each do |c|
            if layout[r][c] == 1
                directions.each_with_index do |dir, dir_idx|
                    length = dfs(layout, r, c, dir_idx, directions, 0, false)
                    max_length = [max_length, length].max
                end
            end
        end
    end
    
    max_length
end

def dfs(layout, r, c, dir_idx, directions, step, turned)
    rows = layout.length
    cols = layout[0].length
    
    return 0 if r < 0 || r >= rows || c < 0 || c >= cols
    
    expected_val = get_expected_value(step)
    return 0 if layout[r][c] != expected_val
    
    dr, dc = directions[dir_idx]
    nr = r + dr
    nc = c + dc
    
    max_length = 1
    
    if nr >= 0 && nr < rows && nc >= 0 && nc < cols
        next_expected = get_expected_value(step + 1)
        if layout[nr][nc] == next_expected
            length = 1 + dfs(layout, nr, nc, dir_idx, directions, step + 1, turned)
            max_length = [max_length, length].max
        end
    end
    
    if !turned
        new_dir_idx = (dir_idx + 1) % 4
        dr2, dc2 = directions[new_dir_idx]
        nr2 = r + dr2
        nc2 = c + dc2
        
        if nr2 >= 0 && nr2 < rows && nc2 >= 0 && nc2 < cols
            next_expected = get_expected_value(step + 1)
            if layout[nr2][nc2] == next_expected
                length = 1 + dfs(layout, nr2, nc2, new_dir_idx, directions, step + 1, true)
                max_length = [max_length, length].max
            end
        end
    end
    
    max_length
end

def get_expected_value(step)
    return 1 if step == 0
    return step % 2 == 1 ? 2 : 0
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def computeLongestZRoute(layout)
    return 0 if layout.empty? || layout[0].empty?
    
    rows = layout.length
    cols = layout[0].length
    directions = [[-1, -1], [-1, 1], [1, 1], [1, -1]]
    
    memo = Array.new(4) { Array.new(rows) { Array.new(cols) { Array.new(2) } } }
    
    dp = lambda do |r, c, d_idx, expected|
      return 0 if r < 0 || r >= rows || c < 0 || c >= cols || layout[r][c] != expected
      val_idx = expected == 2 ? 0 : 1
      return memo[d_idx][r][c][val_idx] if memo[d_idx][r][c][val_idx]
      
      dr, dc = directions[d_idx]
      next_expected = expected == 2 ? 0 : 2
      memo[d_idx][r][c][val_idx] = 1 + dp.call(r + dr, c + dc, d_idx, next_expected)
    end

    max_len = 0
    
    (0...rows).each do |r|
      (0...cols).each do |c|
        if layout[r][c] == 1
          max_len = [max_len, 1].max
          directions.each_with_index do |(dr, dc), d_idx|
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            len1 = dp.call(nr, nc, d_idx, 2)
            if len1 > 0
              max_len = [max_len, 1 + len1].max
              
              turn_d_idx = (d_idx + 1) % 4
              tr, tc = directions[turn_d_idx]
              
              (1..len1).each do |step|
                curr_r = nr + (step - 1) * dr
                curr_c = nc + (step - 1) * dc
                curr_val = layout[curr_r][curr_c]
                
                next_val = curr_val == 2 ? 0 : 2
                len2 = dp.call(curr_r + tr, curr_c + tc, turn_d_idx, next_val)
                max_len = [max_len, 1 + step + len2].max
              end
            end
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    max_len
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.strip.empty?
    return
  end
  
  begin
    layout = JSON.parse(input_data)
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.computeLongestZRoute(layout)
  rescue JSON::ParserError

      cleaned_input = input_data.gsub('layout = ', '').strip

def search_accurate_duo(signal)
  counts = Hash.new(0)
  signal.each_char { |c| counts[c] += 1 }
  
  (0...signal.length - 1).each do |i|
    first = signal[i]
    second = signal[i + 1]
    
    next if first == second
    
    if counts[first] == first.to_i && counts[second] == second.to_i
      return first + second
    end
  end
  
  ""
end

    canvases.times do
        expense << gets.strip.split.map(&:to_i)
    end
    
    n = canvases
    num_pairs = n / 2
    
    INF = Float::INFINITY
    
    dp = Array.new(3) { INF }
    
    for style1 in 0..2
        for style2 in 0..2
            next if style1 == style2
            cost = expense[0][style1] + expense[n-1][style2]
            dp[style1] = [dp[style1], cost].min
        end
    end
    
    for pair in 1...num_pairs
        left_idx = pair
        right_idx = n - 1 - pair
        
        new_dp = Array.new(3) { INF }
        
        for prev_style in 0..2
            next if dp[prev_style] == INF
            
            for style1 in 0..2
                next if style1 == prev_style
                
                for style2 in 0..2
                    next if style1 == style2
                    
                    cost = dp[prev_style] + expense[left_idx][style1] + expense[right_idx][style2]
                    new_dp[style1] = [new_dp[style1], cost].min
                end
            end
        end
        
        dp = new_dp
    end
    
    puts dp.min
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def computeLeastExpense(canvases, expense)
    n = canvases
    m = n / 2
    inf = Float::INFINITY
    
    dp = Array.new(3) { Array.new(3, inf) }
    
    (0..2).each do |j1|
      (0..2).each do |j2|
        if j1 != j2
          dp[j1][j2] = expense[m - 1][j1] + expense[m][j2]
        end
      end
    end
    
    (1...m).each do |k|
      idx_left = m - 1 - k
      idx_right = m + k
      new_dp = Array.new(3) { Array.new(3, inf) }
      
      (0..2).each do |prev_l|
        (0..2).each do |prev_r|
          if dp[prev_l][prev_r] == inf
            next
          end
          
          (0..2).each do |curr_l|
            next if curr_l == prev_l
            (0..2).each do |curr_r|
              next if curr_r == prev_r
              next if curr_l == curr_r
              
              cost = dp[prev_l][prev_r] + expense[idx_left][curr_l] + expense[idx_right][curr_r]
              if cost < new_dp[curr_l][curr_r]
                new_dp[curr_l][curr_r] = cost
              end
            end
          end
        end
      end
      dp = new_dp
    end
    
    ans = inf
    (0..2).each do |j1|
      (0..2).each do |j2|
        ans = dp[j1][j2] if dp[j1][j2] < ans
      end
    end
    
    ans.to_i
  end
end

def main
  input_data = $stdin.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    data = input_data.scan(/canvases\s*=\s*(\d+)|expense\s*=\s*(\[\[.*?\]\])/m)
    canvases = data[0][0].to_i
    expense_str = data[1][1]
    expense = JSON.parse(expense_str)
    
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.computeLeastExpense(canvases, expense)
  rescue
    lines = input_data.split("\n")
    return if lines.empty?
    canvases = lines[0].to_i
    expense = JSON.parse(lines[1])
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.computeLeastExpense(canvases, expense)
  end
end

import sys

class Solution:
    def computeExhibitDistance(self, M: int, N: int, L: int) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        
        def nCr_mod(n, r):
            if r < 0 or r > n:
                return 0
            if r == 0 or r == n:
                return 1
            if r > n
                r = n - r
            
            num = 1
            for i in range(r):
                num = (num * (n - i)) % MOD
            
            den = 1
            for i in range(1, r + 1):
                den = (den * i) % MOD
            
            return (num * pow(den, MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD

        total_cells = M * N
        ways_to_place_others = nCr_mod(total_cells - 2, L - 2)
        
        sum_row_dist = 0
        for d in range(1, M):
            count = (M - d) * N * N
            sum_row_dist = (sum_row_dist + d * count) % MOD
            
        sum_col_dist = 0
        for d in range(1, N):
            count = (N - d) * M * M
            sum_col_dist = (sum_col_dist + d * count) % MOD
            
        total_dist_between_pairs = (sum_row_dist + sum_col_dist) % MOD
        result = (total_dist_between_pairs * ways_to_place_others) % MOD
        return result

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    M = int(input_data[0])
    N = int(input_data[1])
    L = int(input_data[2])
    
    sol = Solution()
    print(sol.computeExhibitDistance(M, N, L))

if __name__ == "__main__":

MOD = 1_000_000_007

def power(a, b)
  res = 1
  a %= MOD
  while b > 0
    res = (res * a) % MOD if b % 2 == 1
    a = (a * a) % MOD
    b /= 2
  end
  res
end

def inverse(n)
  power(n, MOD - 2)
end

class Solution
  def computeExhibitDistance(m, n, l)
    fact = Array.new(m * n + 1)
    inv_fact = Array.new(m * n + 1)
    fact[0] = 1
    (1..m * n).each { |i| fact[i] = (fact[i - 1] * i) % MOD }
    inv_fact[m * n] = inverse(fact[m * n])
    (m * n - 1).downto(0) { |i| inv_fact[i] = (inv_fact[i + 1] * (i + 1)) % MOD }

    def nCr(n, r, fact, inv_fact)
      return 0 if r < 0 || r > n
      num = fact[n]
      den = (inv_fact[r] * inv_fact[n - r]) % MOD
      (num * den) % MOD
    end

    total_dist = 0
    
    sum_row_diffs = 0
    (1..m).each do |d|
      count = (m - d) * n * n
      sum_row_diffs = (sum_row_diffs + d * count) % MOD
    end
    
    sum_col_diffs = 0
    (1..n).each do |d|
      count = (n - d) * m * m
      sum_col_diffs = (sum_col_diffs + d * count) % MOD
    end
    
    total_pair_dist = (sum_row_diffs + sum_col_diffs) % MOD
    ways_to_place_others = nCr(m * n - 2, l - 2, fact, inv_fact)
    
    (total_pair_dist * ways_to_place_others) % MOD
  end
end

def main
  input = STDIN.read.split.map(&:to_i)
  return if input.empty?
  m = input[0]
  n = input[1]
  l = input[2]
  sol = Solution.new
  puts sol.computeExhibitDistance(m, n, l)
end

    shift_duration = gets.to_i
    k = gets.to_i
    n = gets.to_i
    start_slots = []
    end_slots = []
    n.times do
        start_slots << gets.to_i
    end
    n.times do
        end_slots << gets.to_i
    end
    
    if n == 0
        puts shift_duration
        return
    end
    
    appointments = []
    n.times do |i|
        appointments << [start_slots[i], end_slots[i]]
    end
    
    max_idle = 0
    
    (0..n).each do |gap_idx|
        left_adjust = [gap_idx, k].min
        right_adjust = [n - gap_idx, k].min
        
        (0..[left_adjust, right_adjust].min).each do |left_k|
            right_k = [k - left_k, right_adjust].min
            
            left_bound = 0
            if gap_idx > 0
                if gap_idx - left_k - 1 >= 0
                    left_bound = end_slots[gap_idx - left_k - 1]
                else
                    left_bound = 0
                end
            end
            
            right_bound = shift_duration
            if gap_idx < n
                if gap_idx + right_k < n
                    right_bound = start_slots[gap_idx + right_k]
                else
                    right_bound = shift_duration
                end
            end
            
            total_duration = 0
            (gap_idx - left_k...gap_idx).each do |i|
                if i >= 0
                    total_duration += end_slots[i] - start_slots[i]
                end
            end
            
            (gap_idx...[gap_idx + right_k, n].min).each do |i|
                total_duration += end_slots[i] - start_slots[i]
            end
            
            idle = right_bound - left_bound - total_duration
            max_idle = [max_idle, idle].max
        end
    end
    
    puts max_idle
end

class Scheduler
  def max_idle_time(shift_duration, k, start_slots, end_slots)
    n = start_slots.length
    durations = Array.new(n)
    n.times { |i| durations[i] = end_slots[i] - start_slots[i] }
    
    max_idle = 0
    
    (0..n).each do |i|
      (i..n).each do |j|
        gap_size = j - i
        if gap_size <= k
          left_sum = 0
          (0...i).each { |idx| left_sum += durations[idx] }
          
          right_sum = 0
          (j...n).each { |idx| right_sum += durations[idx] }
          
          max_idle = [max_idle, shift_duration - left_sum - right_sum].max
        end
      end
    end
    
    if k > 0
      (0...n).each do |i|
        left_sum = 0
        (0...i).each { |idx| left_sum += durations[idx] }
        max_idle = [max_idle, shift_duration - durations[i] - left_sum].max
        
        right_sum = 0
        ((i+1)...n).each { |idx| right_sum += durations[idx] }
        max_idle = [max_idle, shift_duration - durations[i] - right_sum].max
      end
    end

    max_idle
  end
end

  puts scheduler.max_idle_time(shift_duration, k, start_slots, end_slots)

require 'json'

class AdjustmentCalculator
  def min_adjustments(events)
    total_adjustments = 0
    events.each do |start_val, end_val|
      counts = Hash.new(0)
      curr_start = start_val
      curr_end = end_val
      
      while curr_end > 0
        s = curr_start
        e = curr_end
        if e >= s
          num_elements = e - s + 1
          level = 0
          temp_e = e
          while temp_e > 0
            temp_e /= 4
            level += 1
          end
          
          total_elements_at_this_level = 0
          current_level_val = 0
          
          s_copy = s
          e_copy = e
          
          while s_copy <= e_copy
            count = e_copy - s_copy + 1
            total_adjustments += (count + 1) / 2
            
            new_s = (s_copy / 4.0).floor
            new_e = (e_copy / 4.0).floor
            
            if new_e == 0
              break
            end
            
            s_copy = [1, new_s].max
            e_copy = new_e
          end
        end
        curr_end = 0
      end
    end
    total_adjustments
  end

  def solve_optimized(events)
    total = 0
    events.each do |s, e|
      non_zeros = 0
      current_range = [[s, e]]
      
      while !current_range.empty?
        next_range = []
        current_range.each do |rs, re|
          count = re - rs + 1
          total += (count + 1) / 2
          
          ns = rs / 4
          ne = re / 4
          
          if ne > 0
            actual_ns = [1, ns].max
            if actual_ns <= ne
              next_range << [actual_ns, ne]
            end
          end
        end
        
        if next_range.empty?
          current_range = []
        else
          merged = []
          next_range.sort_by! { |r| r[0] }
          curr = next_range[0]
          (1...next_range.length).each do |i|
            if next_range[i][0] <= curr[1] + 1
              curr[1] = [curr[1], next_range[i][1]].max
            else
              merged << curr
              curr = next_range[i]
            end
          end
          merged << curr
          current_range = merged
        end
      end
    end
    total
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
    return
  end
  
  begin
    events = JSON.parse(input_data)
    calc = AdjustmentCalculator.new
    puts calc.solve_optimized(events)
  rescue
  end
end

class Solution:
    def findMaxSensorSwing(self, readings: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(readings)
        max_diff = 0
        for i in range(n):
            next_i = (i + 1) % n
            diff = abs(readings[i] - readings[next_i])
            max_diff = max(max_diff, diff)
        return max_diff

from typing import List

class TournamentOrganizer:
    def arrangeGrid(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        n = len(grid)
        if n == 0:
            return grid
        
        for k in range(n):
            diagonal = []
            r, c = k, 0
            while r < n and c < n:
                diagonal.append(grid[r][c])
                r += 1
                c += 1
            diagonal.sort(reverse=True)
            r, c = k, 0
            idx = 0
            while r < n and c < n:
                grid[r][c] = diagonal[idx]
                r += 1
                c += 1
                idx += 1
                
        for k in range(1, n):
            diagonal = []
            r, c = 0, k
            while r < n and c < n:
                diagonal.append(grid[r][c])
                r += 1
                c += 1
            diagonal.sort()
            r, c = 0, k
            idx = 0
            while r < n and c < n:
                grid[r][c] = diagonal[idx]
                r += 1
                c += 1
                idx += 1
                
        return grid

require 'json'

class TournamentOrganizer
  def arrangeGrid(grid)
    n = grid.length
    diagonals = {}

    (0...n).each do |r|
      (0...n).each do |c|
        diff = r - c
        diagonals[diff] ||= []
        diagonals[diff] << grid[r][c]
      end
    end

    diagonals.each do |diff, values|
      if diff >= 0
        values.sort! { |a, b| b <=> a }
      else
        values.sort!
      end
    end

    result = Array.new(n) { Array.new(n) }
    indices = Hash.new(0)

    (0...n).each do |r|
      (0...n).each do |c|
        diff = r - c
        result[r][c] = diagonals[diff][indices[diff]]
        indices[diff] += 1
      end
    end

    result
  end
end

class Solution:
    def processRequests(self, containers: List[int], requests: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        n = len(containers)
        result = []
        
        for req_idx in requests:
            target_code = containers[req_idx]
            min_dist = float('inf')
            found = False
            
            for i in range(n):
                if i != req_idx and containers[i] == target_code:
                    found = True
                    forward_dist = (i - req_idx) % n
                    backward_dist = (req_idx - i) % n
                    dist = min(forward_dist, backward_dist)
                    min_dist = min(min_dist, dist)
            
            if found:
                result.append(min_dist)
            else:
                result.append(-1)
        
        return result

def process_requests(containers, requests)
    n = containers.length
    results = []
    
    requests.each do |req_idx|
        target_code = containers[req_idx]
        min_dist = -1
        
        (1...n).each do |offset|
            right_idx = (req_idx + offset) % n
            left_idx = (req_idx - offset) % n
            
            if containers[right_idx] == target_code
                min_dist = offset
                break
            end
            
            if containers[left_idx] == target_code
                min_dist = offset
                break
            end
        end
        
        results << min_dist
    end
    
    results
end

import sys
import collections

class Solution:
    def processRequests(self, containers, requests):
        n = len(containers)
        pos_map = collections.defaultdict(list)
        for i, val in enumerate(containers):
            pos_map[val].append(i)
        
        memo = {}
        results = []
        for req_idx in requests:
            val = containers[req_idx]
            if val in memo:
                results.append(memo[val])
                continue
            
            indices = pos_map[val]
            if len(indices) <= 1:
                memo[val] = -1
                results.append(-1)
                continue
            
            min_dist = n
            m = len(indices)
            
            import bisect
            idx_in_list = bisect.bisect_left(indices, req_idx)
            
            prev_idx = indices[(idx_in_list - 1) % m]
            next_idx = indices[(idx_in_list + 1) % m]
            
            d1 = (req_idx - prev_idx) % n
            d2 = (next_idx - req_idx) % n
            
            res = min(d1, d2)
            memo[val] = res
            results.append(res)
            
        return results

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    ptr = 0
    while ptr < len(input_data):
        try:
            n = int(input_data[ptr])
            ptr += 1
            containers = []
            for _ in range(n):
                containers.append(int(input_data[ptr]))
                ptr += 1
            
            r_count = int(input_data[ptr])
            ptr += 1
            requests = []
            for _ in range(r_count):

import json
import sys

class Solution:
    def processRequests(self, containers, requests):
        n = len(containers)
        pos_map = {}
        for i, val in enumerate(containers):
            if val not in pos_map:
                pos_map[val] = []
            pos_map[val].append(i)
        
        results = []
        for idx in requests:
            code = containers[idx]
            indices = pos_map[code]
            if len(indices) <= 1:
                results.append(-1)
                continue
            
            import bisect
            pos = bisect.bisect_left(indices, idx)
            
            min_dist = float('inf')
            
            left_idx = indices[pos - 1]
            dist_left = (idx - left_idx) % n
            if dist_left < min_dist:
                min_dist = dist_left
                
            right_idx = indices[(pos + 1) % len(indices)]
            dist_right = (right_idx - idx) % n
            if dist_right < min_dist:
                min_dist = dist_right
            
            results.append(min_dist)
        return results

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    try:
        data = json.loads(input_data)
        containers = data.get("containers")
        requests = data.get("requests")
        
        sol = Solution()
        output = sol.processRequests(containers, requests)
        print(json.dumps(output))
    except:
        lines = input_data.strip().split('\n')
        if len(lines) >= 2:
            containers = json.loads(lines[0].split('=')[-1].strip())
            requests = json.loads(lines[1].split('=')[-1].strip())
            sol = Solution()
            output = sol.processRequests(containers, requests)
            print(json.dumps(output))

def solve()
  input_data = $stdin.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  scores_match = input_data.match(/scores\s*=\s*(\[[^\]]*\])/)
  target_match = input_data.match(/target\s*=\s*(\d+)/)
  
  return if scores_match.nil? || target_match.nil?
  
  scores = JSON.parse(scores_match[1])
  target = target_match[1].to_i
  
  n = scores.length
  prefix_target = Array.new(n + 1, 0)
  (0...n).each do |i|
    prefix_target[i + 1] = prefix_target[i] + (scores[i] == target ? 1 : 0)
  end
  
  total_target = prefix_target[n]
  max_occurrence = total_target
  
  unique_vals = scores.uniq
  unique_vals.each do |v|
    next if v == target
    
    delta = target - v
    current_diff = 0
    min_diff = 0
    
    scores.each_with_index do |score, i|
      val = (score == v ? 1 : (score == target ? -1 : 0))
      current_diff += val
      
      gain = current_diff - min_diff
      if total_target + gain > max_occurrence
        max_occurrence = total_target + gain
      end
      
      if current_diff < min_diff
        min_diff = current_diff
      end
    end
  end
  
  puts max_occurrence
end

class Solution
  def computeMaxDisparity(seq)
    counts = Hash.new(0)
    seq.each_char { |char| counts[char] += 1 }
    
    odds = []
    evens = []
    
    counts.each_value do |v|
      if v % 2 == 0
        evens << v
      else
        odds << v
      end
    end
    
    max_disparity = -1000000
    odds.each do |o|
      evens.each do |e|
        diff = o - e
        max_disparity = diff if diff > max_disparity
      end
    end
    
    max_disparity
  end
end

class Navigator
    def maxDisplacement(commands, k)
        n = commands.length
        max_dist = 0
        
        (0..n).each do |i|
            max_dist = [max_dist, max_dist_from_position(commands, k, i)].max
        end
        
        max_dist
    end
    
    def max_dist_from_position(commands, k, pos)
        x = 0
        y = 0
        
        (0...pos).each do |i|
            case commands[i]
            when 'N'
                y += 1
            when 'S'
                y -= 1
            when 'E'
                x += 1
            when 'W'
                x -= 1
            end
        end
        
        changes_needed = 0
        best_changes = []
        
        (pos...commands.length).each do |i|
            cmd = commands[i]
            
            dx = 0
            dy = 0
            case cmd
            when 'N'
                dy = 1
            when 'S'
                dy = -1
            when 'E'
                dx = 1
            when 'W'
                dx = -1
            end
            
            best_dx = x >= 0 ? 1 : -1
            best_dy = y >= 0 ? 1 : -1
            
            current_gain = dx * (x >= 0 ? 1 : -1) + dy * (y >= 0 ? 1 : -1)
            best_gain = best_dx * (x >= 0 ? 1 : -1) + best_dy * (y >= 0 ? 1 : -1)
            
            if current_gain < best_gain
                best_changes << [i, best_gain - current_gain, best_dx, best_dy]
            end
        end
        
        best_changes.sort_by! { |change| -change[1] }
        
        changed = {}
        best_changes.take(k).each do |change|
            changed[change[0]] = [change[2], change[3]]
        end
        
        (pos...commands.length).each do |i|
            if changed.key?(i)
                x += changed[i][0]
                y += changed[i][1]
            else
                case commands[i]
                when 'N'
                    y += 1
                when 'S'
                    y -= 1
                when 'E'
                    x += 1
                when 'W'
                    x -= 1
                end
            end
        end
        
        x.abs + y.abs
    end
end

import sys

class Navigator:
    def maxDisplacement(self, commands: str, k: int) -> int:
        n = len(commands)
        res = 0
        
        for dx, dy in [(1, 1), (1, -1), (-1, 1), (-1, -1)]:
            costs = []
            for char in commands:
                if char == 'N':
                    cx, cy = 0, 1
                elif char == 'S':
                    cx, cy = 0, -1
                elif char == 'E':
                    cx, cy = 1, 0
                else:
                    cx, cy = -1, 0
                
                current_val = cx * dx + cy * dy
                costs.append(2 - current_val)
            
            prefix_sum = 0
            sorted_costs = []
            
            import heapq
            min_heap = []
            current_cost_sum = 0
            
            for i in range(n):
                if char == 'N': cx, cy = 0, 1
                elif char == 'S': cx, cy = 0, -1
                elif char == 'E': cx, cy = 1, 0
                else: cx, cy = -1, 0
                
                char = commands[i]
                if char == 'N': cur_val = 0 * dx + 1 * dy
                elif char == 'S': cur_val = 0 * dx + -1 * dy
                elif char == 'E': cur_val = 1 * dx + 0 * dy
                else: cur_val = -1 * dx + 0 * dy
                
                prefix_sum += cur_val
                cost = 2 - cur_val
                heapq.heappush(min_heap, cost)
                current_cost_sum += cost
                
                while len(min_heap) > k:
                    current_cost_sum -= heapq.heappop(min_heap)
                
                res = max(res, prefix_sum + current_cost_sum)
                
        return int(res)

class Navigator
  def maxDisplacement(commands, k)
    n = commands.length
    max_dist = 0
    
    4.times do |mask|
      target_x = (mask & 1 == 0) ? 'E' : 'W'
      target_y = (mask & 2 == 0) ? 'N' : 'S'
      
      x, y = 0, 0
      used_k = 0
      
      commands.each_char do |c|
        if c == target_x || c == target_y
          if c == 'N' then y += 1
          elsif c == 'S' then y -= 1
          elsif c == 'E' then x += 1
          elsif c == 'W' then x -= 1
          end
        elsif used_k < k
          used_k += 1
          if target_x == 'E' && (c == 'W' || (target_y == 'N' && c == 'S') || (target_y == 'S' && c == 'N'))
             x += 1
          elsif target_x == 'W'
             x -= 1
          elsif target_y == 'N'
             y += 1
          else
             y -= 1
          end
        else
          if c == 'N' then y += 1
          elsif c == 'S' then y -= 1
          elsif c == 'E' then x += 1
          elsif c == 'W' then x -= 1
          end
        end
        max_dist = [max_dist, x.abs + y.abs].max
      end
    end

    directions = [['N', 'S'], ['E', 'W']]
    directions.each do |pos, neg|
      [pos, neg].each do |favored|
        curr_k = 0
        dist = 0
        commands.each_char do |c|
          if c == favored
            dist += 1
          elsif curr_k < k
            dist += 1
            curr_k += 1
          else
            dist -= 1
          end
          max_dist = [max_dist, dist.abs].max
        end
      end
    end

    max_dist
  end
end

class RouteOptimizer:
    def computeCargoProfit(self, consumptionRates: List[int], cargoValues: List[int], k: int) -> List[int]:
        n = len(consumptionRates)
        indices = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: consumptionRates[i])
        
        results = [0] * n
        min_heap = []
        current_sum = 0
        
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            j = i
            while j < n and consumptionRates[indices[j]] == consumptionRates[indices[i]]:
                idx = indices[j]
                results[idx] = current_sum
                j += 1
            
            for m in range(i, j):
                idx = indices[m]
                val = cargoValues[idx]
                heapq.heappush(min_heap, val)
                current_sum += val
                if len(min_heap) > k:
                    current_sum -= heapq.heappop(min_heap)
            
            i = j
            
        return results

require 'json'

class RouteOptimizer
  def computeCargoProfit(consumption_rates, cargo_values, k)
    n = consumption_rates.length
    indexed_data = []
    n.times { |i| indexed_data << [consumption_rates[i], cargo_values[i], i] }
    indexed_data.sort_by! { |item| item[0] }

    results = Array.new(n, 0)
    min_heap = MinHeap.new
    current_sum = 0
    
    i = 0
    while i < n
      j = i
      while j < n && indexed_data[j][0] == indexed_data[i][0]
        results[indexed_data[j][2]] = current_sum
        j += 1
      end
      
      (i...j).each do |idx|
        val = indexed_data[idx][1]
        min_heap.push(val)
        current_sum += val
        if min_heap.size > k
          current_sum -= min_heap.pop
        end
      end
      i = j
    end
    
    results
  end
end

class MinHeap
  def initialize
    @heap = []
  end

  def push(val)
    @heap << val
    bubble_up(@heap.length - 1)
  end

  def pop
    return nil if @heap.empty?
    return @heap.pop if @heap.length == 1
    
    root = @heap[0]
    @heap[0] = @heap.pop
    bubble_down(0)
    root
  end

  def size
    @heap.length
  end

  private

  def bubble_up(index)
    parent = (index - 1) / 2
    if index > 0 && @heap[index] < @heap[parent]
      @heap[index], @heap[parent] = @heap[parent], @heap[index]
      bubble_up(parent)
    end
  end

  def bubble_down(index)
    child = index * 2 + 1
    return if child >= @heap.length
    
    right = child + 1
    child = right if right < @heap.length && @heap[right] < @heap[child]
    
    if @heap[child] < @heap[index]
      @heap[index], @heap[child] = @heap[child], @heap[index]
      bubble_down(child)
    end
  end
end
